title: Authentication: password hashing + JWT/session + protected routes
when: Any app with user accounts, login, or protected resources
principle: NEVER store plaintext passwords — hash with bcrypt/argon2 (salted). Issue a signed token (JWT) or server session on login; verify it on every protected route via middleware/dependency. Set short token expiry + refresh. Validate and rate-limit login. Scope access with roles (RBAC). Never trust client-supplied identity.
exemplar: def check_pw(pw, h): return bcrypt.checkpw(pw.encode(), h.encode())
except jwt.PyJWTError: raise PermissionError('invalid or expired token')
# protect: uid = require_auth(bearer_token, SECRET)  # 401 on failure
pitfalls: Plaintext or unsalted passwords, no token expiry, trusting a client-sent user id, secrets hardcoded in source, no rate limiting on login.
